dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3272 IS: Motorcycle Safety Awareness Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Peters (for himself and Ms. Ernst) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To amend title 23, United States Code, with respect to motorcyclist safety, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Motorcycle Safety Awareness Act of 2025.

subsection.text: Section 405(f)(3) of title 23, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking at least 2 of the following criteria and inserting at least 3 of the following criteria, 1 of which shall be the criterion described in subparagraph (H); and
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.header: Driver education and driving safety courses
subparagraph.text: The inclusion, in driver education and driver safety courses provided to individuals by educational and motor vehicle agencies of the State, of instruction and testing relating to motorcyclist awareness, including information relating to—

clause.text: State-specific motorcycle laws, such as lane-splitting and lane-filtering laws; and
share-the-road principles intended to increase awareness of motorcyclists and scooter riders on the roadway.

subsection.text: The amendments made by subsection (a) shall take effect on the date that is 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act.
